Hero.name: Nycolas Souza
Hero.tagline: When an idea makes sense, I like seeing how far it goes.

capo.title: CAPO
capo.description: A system that tracks parts through an industrial pipe factory — cutting, assembly, welding — releasing the next stage automatically as soon as the last one's done.
highlights: Each operator only sees the stage of their own role — cutting, assembly, or welding.
The next stage unlocks by itself as soon as the previous one's done, with no duplicated data.
Updates in real time: the next station sees the new order without reloading the page.

speedy.title: Speedy
speedy.description: An autonomous race car that reads the track through a camera, dodges obstacles with LiDAR, and holds its course on its own, running on a Raspberry Pi.
highlights: Fuses camera, LiDAR and motion sensors to know exactly where it is and where it's headed.
Recognizes signs and obstacles on the track with a vision model trained from scratch.
Runs entirely on a Raspberry Pi 4, no external computer needed.

wash-buddy.title: Wash Buddy
wash-buddy.description: A robot that turns handwashing into a game for kids, with animated expressions, sound, and a card that recognizes who's washing up.
highlights: An RFID card recognizes who's washing their hands.
The animated face on the display changes expression as the wash cycle progresses.
Its own circuit board shuts itself off when idle, so it never wastes power on standby.

world-of-toilets.title: World of Toilets
world-of-toilets.description: An app that helps you find nearby public restrooms, with routes calculated by its own AI service and infrastructure built to never go down.
highlights: Mobile and web apps share the same backend, with routes computed by its own AI service.
Container infrastructure with a 3-node replicated database, built to never go down.
Fine-grained login permissions and protection against the most common attacks.

physics-simulator.title: Physics Simulator
physics-simulator.description: A desktop particle physics simulator where you can watch acceleration, force and gravity play out, with a camera that follows the motion.
highlights: Two modes: constant acceleration, or full physics with configurable force and gravity.
Camera with automatic zoom and a mode that follows the particle in motion.
Saves and reloads entire simulations through its own project files.
